name: Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra
description: Samsung's ultimate flagship — a 6.9-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X display, Snapdragon 8 Elite processor, a 200 MP main camera with 100× Space Zoom, and the redesigned integrated S Pen. Built for power users who demand the best of Android without compromise.

specifications.value: 6.9-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X, 1–120 Hz, 3088 × 1440 px, 2600 nits peak
200 MP main (f/1.7) + 12 MP ultrawide + 10 MP telephoto 3× + 50 MP telephoto 5× (100× Space Zoom)
Android 15 with One UI 7, 7 years of OS updates
Integrated S Pen (2nd generation, BLE-free, 2.8 ms latency)
Corning Gorilla Armor 2 glass, titanium frame
Titanium Silverblue, Titanium Black, Titanium Whitesilver, Titanium Gray

additionalInfo.value: Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd., 129 Samsung-ro, Yeongtong-gu, Suwon-si, Korea
Galaxy S25 Ultra, USB-C cable, S Pen, ejection pin, documentation

reviews.title: The absolute best phone money can buy
reviews.reviewText: Three years as a Note-series fan and the S25 Ultra continues the legacy brilliantly. The 200 MP sensor captures insane detail and the 100× zoom shots are actually usable. S Pen feels more responsive than ever. This phone does everything.

reviews.title: S Pen is still a game-changer
reviews.reviewText: I take handwritten notes all day in meetings and the zero-latency S Pen on this screen is unmatched. The display is the best I've seen on any mobile device — vibrant, sharp, and perfectly regulated at any brightness level.

reviews.title: Beast of a phone with one caveat
reviews.reviewText: Snapdragon 8 Elite absolutely screams. Gaming, video editing, 8K shooting — it handles everything without a sweat. The only caveat is the price: at $1,299 it's a significant investment. If you can afford it though, there's nothing better on Android.

reviews.title: Zoom camera is legitimately impressive
reviews.reviewText: I photographed a football match from the stands and the 50× zoom shots looked like I had a press-pass on the pitch. Truly remarkable hardware. One UI 7 is also the most polished Samsung software has ever been.

reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.reviewText: I debated this purchase for two months given the price. After three weeks of daily use I have zero regrets. The display, performance, camera, and S Pen integration make this the most productive phone I've ever owned.
